message:
fix(doctor): distinguish ssh transport failures from 'path missing'

`check_remote_path`'s `Ok(_)` branch previously folded every non-zero
ssh exit into "Path does not exist" with a "Remove this path or create
it on the remote" remediation. If ssh couldn't even reach the host
(connection refused, permission denied, host key verification failed,
hostname unresolvable, network unreachable, etc.), the operator would
see that remediation text, potentially delete a valid path from their
config, and end up further from a working sync rather than closer.

Inspect stderr for the usual ssh(1) transport-level error signatures
and route those cases to a "warn" with a remediation pointing at ssh
configuration / credentials instead. Genuine remote `test -d` failures
(stderr clean, exit code from the remote shell) still map to the
original "fail" + "Remove or create" remediation, so the existing
diagnostic remains accurate when the path actually is missing.

Adjacent to — but independent of — the #190 fix in this file: that one
made `check_remote_path` actually reach the remote shell correctly;
this one makes sure we report accurately when the remote shell can't
be reached at all.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
